text: Cultural ADD — the collective shortening of public patience for extended argument — is not merely an individual failure of discipline. Media theorist Neil Postman argued in "Amusing Ourselves to Death" that every medium carries an implicit epistemology: television privileged the entertaining over the coherent; its successor platforms intensify this logic. When a political debate must fit a thirty-second clip, when a scientific finding must resolve into a tweet, the very syntax of public reasoning is reshaped. Ideas that require qualifications, exceptions, or extended chains of evidence become structurally disadvantaged in a format-constrained information ecosystem.
question: According to the passage, what did Neil Postman argue every medium carries?

options.text: A preference for visual over verbal content
